rationale: harness/system_prompt.txt:17-18 is a pointer to harness/skills/, not an inventory. On a context-window-constrained local model (e.g. MiniMax-M3) the agent burns its first turn list_dir-ing the skills directory before any productive work. Enumerating each manifest skill name (sorted ascending, no .json suffix) in the prompt removes that first-turn tool call and the latency it costs. The existing pointer line is retained (out of scope to drop it) and only the names are embedded, not the full input_schema. NOTE: per AGENTS.md section 2 and ADR-0004 this file is a ProposedEdit artifact, NOT a direct edit. harness/system_prompt.txt is agent DNA evolved only through the Evolver->Critic gate; this proposal must pass the Critic benchmark gate (ADR-0004) and receive a second human reviewer before it is applied.
old_text:
You have access to the tool surface declared in `harness/skills/`. Respect
the schema of each skill exactly; do not invent new tools.
new_text:
You have access to the tool surface declared in `harness/skills/`. Respect
the schema of each skill exactly; do not invent new tools. The current
skills are:

- bash
- edit_file
- example_skill
- grep_search
- list_dir
- write_file
